texto: I've criticized President Bush for his failure to use his veto pen. There's plenty of blame to go around. The question is how to solve problems. It's not bailouts. What made America great? Free markets, free enterprise, manufacturing, job creation. That's how we're gonna do it, not by enlarging government.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Scott Brown que provoca temas ligados a negocios. Ao questionar “I've criticized President Bush for his failure to use his veto pen. There's plenty of blame to go…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre negocios, great, america, com foco em america e government.

frase_original: I've criticized President Bush for his failure to use his veto pen. There's plenty of blame to go around. The question is how to solve problems. It's not bailouts. What made America great? Free markets, free enterprise, manufacturing, job creation. That's how we're gonna do it, not by enlarging government.

texto: Somehow, the greater the public opposition to the health care bill, the more determined they seem to force it on us anyway. Their attitude shows Washington at its very worst - the presumption that they know best, and they're going to get their way whether the American people like it or not.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Scott Brown: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “Somehow, the greater the public opposition to the health care bill, the more determined they seem…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, inspiracional, health pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e health.

frase_original: Somehow, the greater the public opposition to the health care bill, the more determined they seem to force it on us anyway. Their attitude shows Washington at its very worst - the presumption that they know best, and they're going to get their way whether the American people like it or not.
